message:
Harden error paths in distributed, retry, and symbol obligation subsystems

Three independent correctness improvements that replace panics and
implicit assumptions with explicit error handling:

1. Snapshot deserialization (src/distributed/snapshot.rs):
   Replace permissive if/else presence-flag parsing with exhaustive
   match on {0, 1, _} for all optional field readers (read_optional_u64,
   read_optional_u32, read_optional_string, parent field). Invalid flag
   values now return a new InvalidPresenceFlag(u8) error variant instead
   of silently treating any non-1 byte as None. Includes Display/Debug
   impls, exhaustive error-list test updates, and two targeted unit
   tests for corrupt budget and parent presence flags.

2. Retry combinator cancellation (src/combinator/retry.rs):
   Fix premature cancellation of in-flight futures. Previously, when a
   Cx cancellation was requested while the retry combinator was in the
   Polling state, the inner future was force-dropped without being
   drained to Outcome::Cancelled — violating asupersync's structured
   cancellation contract. Now cancellation is only acted upon in Idle
   and Sleeping states; in Polling state the inner future continues to
   be polled so it can observe the cancel signal and drain normally.

3. Symbol obligation tracker (src/record/symbol_obligation_tracker.rs):
   Replace panic!("not a decoding obligation") with a typed
   DecodingProgressUpdateError enum that distinguishes NotDecodingObligation
   from SymbolsReceivedExceedsNeeded, with Display and Error impls.
   update_decoding_progress() now returns Result<(), DecodingProgressUpdateError>.
   Adds two new tests validating both error variants and verifying the
   symbols_received counter is not mutated on rejected updates.

4. Recovery symbol handling (src/distributed/recovery.rs):
   Remove underscore prefix from `reason` variable in rejected symbol
   warning to ensure the rejection reason is actually captured in traces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
